evals.prompt: We need an ADR for choosing PostgreSQL over MongoDB for our user data store
evals.expected_output: Creates a new ADR file in docs/adrs/ using MADR format with PostgreSQL vs MongoDB as considered options

assertions.text: ADR file created at docs/adrs/ADR-XXXX-*.md with correct sequential numbering
YAML frontmatter contains status: proposed
At least 2 considered options listed (PostgreSQL and MongoDB minimum)
Pros and cons section exists for each considered option
Decision Outcome section present with chosen option and justification
Mermaid diagram included in Architecture Diagram section
Suggests next step: /design:spec

evals.prompt: Create an ADR about using gRPC for inter-service communication --module api
evals.expected_output: Creates ADR scoped to the api module's docs/adrs/ directory using artifact path resolution

assertions.text: Artifact path resolution invoked with --module api
ADR file created under the api module's ADR directory, not the project root
YAML frontmatter contains status: proposed and date field
Considered Options section includes gRPC and at least one alternative (e.g., REST, GraphQL)
ADR number is sequential within the module's ADR directory
CLAUDE.md integration check targets the module root, not project root

evals.expected_output: Creates CLAUDE.md with architecture context, detects .gitmodules for workspace setup, offers permission auto-configuration

assertions.text: Checks for .claude-plugin-design.json migration before main init flow
CLAUDE.md created or updated with ## Architecture Context section
CLAUDE.md contains references to docs/adrs/ and docs/openspec/specs/
Offers to configure .claude/settings.json with permission allowlists via AskUserQuestion
Checks for .gitmodules and offers workspace setup if submodules found
Detects backend project manifests (go.mod, package.json, etc.) and suggests structured logging ADR if none exists
Outputs next steps mentioning /design:adr, /design:spec, and /design:prime

evals.expected_output: Detects already-configured CLAUDE.md and reports no changes needed (idempotent re-run)

assertions.text: Reads existing CLAUDE.md and finds both docs/adrs/ and docs/openspec/specs/ references
Reports 'already configured' without modifying the file
Does NOT append a duplicate ## Architecture Context section
Output includes current ADR path and spec path

files.content:
## Architecture Context

This project uses the [design plugin](https://github.com/joestump/claude-plugin-design) for architecture governance.

- Architecture Decision Records are in `docs/adrs/`
- Specifications are in `docs/openspec/specs/`

evals.expected_output: Loads architecture context filtered by 'authentication' topic, showing only semantically relevant ADRs and specs

assertions.text: Checks CLAUDE.md for design plugin references before scanning
Scans ADR and spec directories for artifacts
Applies semantic topic filter — matches ADRs/specs related to auth, sessions, tokens, access control
Output includes 'Matching ADRs' and 'Matching Specs' tables with Relevance column
Output includes 'Skipped' section listing non-matching artifacts
Output includes 'Summaries' section with 2-3 sentence summaries of matching artifacts
No files are modified (read-only skill)

# ADR-0001: Use JWT for API Authentication

## Context and Problem Statement

We need a stateless authentication mechanism for our API.

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: JWT tokens, because they enable stateless auth.

---
status: accepted
date: 2025-07-01
---

# ADR-0002: Use PostgreSQL for Primary Database

## Context and Problem Statement

We need a relational database for user data.

## Decision Outcome

Chosen option: PostgreSQL, because of its reliability.

Does NOT attempt to scan for ADRs or specs

evals.prompt: Organize the issues for SPEC-0005 into a GitHub project --dry-run
evals.expected_output: Previews project creation and issue grouping without making any changes

assertions.text: Resolves SPEC-0005 by reading spec.md from the spec directory
Validates spec pairing (checks both spec.md and design.md exist)
Detects tracker via Config Resolution pattern from CLAUDE.md
Searches tracker for issues referencing SPEC-0005
Presents three-tier intervention choice (leave as-is / restructure workspace / complete refactor)
In --dry-run mode: reports what WOULD be done at each tier without modifying anything
No tracker issues or projects are created or modified

# SPEC-0005: Notification Service

## Overview

Real-time notification delivery system.

### Requirement: Email Notifications

The system MUST support email delivery.

### Requirement: Push Notifications

The system SHOULD support push notifications.

# SPEC-0005: Notification Service — Design

## Architecture

Event-driven notification pipeline.

evals.expected_output: Resolves spec within the worker module's artifact paths and organizes issues scoped to that module

assertions.text: Artifact path resolution invoked with --module worker
Spec resolved from worker module's spec directory, not project root
Validates spec pairing (spec.md + design.md) within the module
Reads tracker config from CLAUDE.md via Config Resolution pattern
Presents three-tier intervention choice via AskUserQuestion
Uses ToolSearch to discover tracker project tools at runtime

evals.prompt: Add branch names and PR conventions to all SPEC-0005 issues
evals.expected_output: Enriches existing tracker issues for SPEC-0005 with ### Branch and ### PR Convention sections

assertions.text: Resolves SPEC-0005 by reading spec.md from the spec directory
Validates spec pairing (checks both spec.md and design.md exist)
Reads Branch Conventions and PR Conventions from CLAUDE.md ### Design Plugin Configuration
Detects tracker via Config Resolution pattern
For each issue: checks if ### Branch section already exists before adding (idempotent)
For each issue: checks if ### PR Convention section already exists before adding (idempotent)
Branch slug derived from issue title in kebab-case, max 50 chars
Epic issues get epic/ prefix, task issues get feature/ prefix
Reports summary: issues enriched, skipped, and any failures
